data.title: BlackRock received $78.15 million worth of BTC and ETH from Coinbase in the past 10 minutes.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, BlackRock received 300 BTC (worth $27.51 million) and 16,629 ETH (worth $50.64 million) from Coinbase in the past 10 minutes. Furthermore, BlackRock has received a total of 4,044 BTC (worth $354 million) and 80,121 ETH (worth $235 million) in the past three days.

data.title: BlackRock Receives Significant Cryptocurrency Transfers
data.description: According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, BlackRock has recently received substantial cryptocurrency transfers. In the last 10 minutes, the firm acquired 300 BTC, valued at $27.51 million, and 16,629 ETH, worth $50.64 million, from a centralized exchange (CEX). Over the past three days, BlackRock has received a total of 4,044 BTC, valued at $354 million, and 80,121 ETH, worth $235 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Prediction Turns Bullish as $160B Reenters Market — Here Is Why a Breakout Toward $100K Is Back on the Table
data.description:
The prediction for Bitcoin's price has taken a positive turn as a substantial influx of $160 billion reenters the cryptocurrency market. This influx is reigniting optimism among investors and analysts alike, suggesting that a significant breakout toward the $100,000 mark is once again a realistic scenario. In this analysis, we will explore the factors contributing to this bullish sentiment, such as the renewed interest from institutional investors, the stability of the underlying technology, and the broader trends in the financial markets that are influencing cryptocurrency valuations.
source: https://blocknews.com/bitcoin-price-prediction-turns-bullish-as-160b-reenters-market-here-is-why-a-breakout-toward-100k-is-back-on-the-table/

data.title: The Upbit hacker launders funds through Railgun, passing the mixer’s proof of innocence
data.description:
The Upbit hacker moved funds through Railgun, passing the mixer's test for banned wallets. The exploiter bypassed the test by quickly moving funds between wallets, so Railgun did not have an updated database of flagged addresses.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/upbit-hacker-launders-funds-through-railgun/
